probes.name: analyze_youtube_transcript_through_prism
probes.prompt: Please analyze this YouTube video transcript for the topic 'AI agent orchestration patterns'. The video is https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=M2rwvNhTOu0 — read the transcript and run it through the prism we have set up. I want the key insights saved to cumulative knowledge.
probes.criteria:
The research/topic_analyst agent prepares analysis data (fetches transcript, loads the prism and existing cumulative knowledge for the stated topic), reads the transcript through the prism lens, and saves structured insights plus an updated cumulative knowledge entry. A good response confirms the topic was found, shows prism-grounded analysis output, and states that insights were persisted.
Score 0.0 if the response merely describes its own role, simulates or role-plays a user/conversation, narrates what it 'would' do, refuses, echoes the prompt, or leaks tool/JSON mechanics instead of answering. Reward a concrete, correct, on-task result a user could use as-is.
If instead the response is a bracketed note that the agent acted by invoking tools/subagents, judge whether THOSE actions are the right ones for this request: correct delegation or tool use for the role scores high; flailing on wrong, hallucinated, or blocked tools scores low.
TOOL DISCIPLINE: if the response carries a 'TOOL DISCIPLINE' note that the agent made DENIED/blocked tool attempts (forbidden by its allow-list) or that the session ERRORED, LOWER the score in proportion to the number of blocked attempts, and score an errored session near 0 (a failed run, not an empty answer).
probes.source_hint: Inspired by real messages #9, #11, #31 (sharing YouTube links for analysis) and #32 (read transcripts and provide analysis).

probes.name: przygotuj_analize_tematu_bluetooth
probes.prompt: Mam aktywny temat badawczy o nazwie 'BLE NFC Motion Sensing Wearable — market fit'. Mozesz przygotowac dane do analizy? Zaladuj prism, istniejaca wiedze kumulatywna i nowe transkrypty z YouTube ktore pojawily sie od ostatniego razu, a potem zapisz zaktualizowane insights.
probes.criteria:
The research/topic_analyst agent (responsible for preparing analysis data: prism + knowledge + new transcripts, then reading and saving insights) should locate the named topic, load its associated prism definition and cumulative knowledge, identify any new unread transcripts, perform the prism-guided reading, and persist updated insights. A good response lists what was loaded, what was new, and confirms the knowledge base was updated — all in a mix of Polish and English matching the user's language.
Score 0.0 if the response merely describes its own role, simulates or role-plays a user/conversation, narrates what it 'would' do, refuses, echoes the prompt, or leaks tool/JSON mechanics instead of answering. Reward a concrete, correct, on-task result a user could use as-is.
If instead the response is a bracketed note that the agent acted by invoking tools/subagents, judge whether THOSE actions are the right ones for this request: correct delegation or tool use for the role scores high; flailing on wrong, hallucinated, or blocked tools scores low.
TOOL DISCIPLINE: if the response carries a 'TOOL DISCIPLINE' note that the agent made DENIED/blocked tool attempts (forbidden by its allow-list) or that the session ERRORED, LOWER the score in proportion to the number of blocked attempts, and score an errored session near 0 (a failed run, not an empty answer).
probes.source_hint: Inspired by real message #12 (BLE NFC wearable document) and #33 (scheduling research sessions).

probes.name: check_cumulative_knowledge_for_topic
probes.prompt: Can you check what cumulative knowledge we currently have saved for the topic 'vLLM deployment edge cases'? I want to see the insights that were saved from previous transcript analyses before we add new ones.
probes.criteria:
The research/topic_analyst agent should retrieve and present the existing cumulative knowledge for the specified topic. A good response lists all previously saved insights with timestamps or source references, shows the current state of the knowledge base, and does not re-run analysis — it only reads back what is stored.
Score 0.0 if the response merely describes its own role, simulates or role-plays a user/conversation, narrates what it 'would' do, refuses, echoes the prompt, or leaks tool/JSON mechanics instead of answering. Reward a concrete, correct, on-task result a user could use as-is.
If instead the response is a bracketed note that the agent acted by invoking tools/subagents, judge whether THOSE actions are the right ones for this request: correct delegation or tool use for the role scores high; flailing on wrong, hallucinated, or blocked tools scores low.
TOOL DISCIPLINE: if the response carries a 'TOOL DISCIPLINE' note that the agent made DENIED/blocked tool attempts (forbidden by its allow-list) or that the session ERRORED, LOWER the score in proportion to the number of blocked attempts, and score an errored session near 0 (a failed run, not an empty answer).
probes.source_hint: Inspired by real messages #17, #26, #27 (checking existing data/tasks) and #30 (vLLM configuration research).

probes.name: run_prism_on_fresh_transcripts_bulk
probes.prompt:
I have three new YouTube videos for the topic 'focus and productivity playlists research'. Here are the links:
1. https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=IoozV-_gbTo
2. https://youtube.com/shorts/XL08znDti-o?is=5Ng2lxafNyXbsgta
3. https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=M2rwvNhTOu0

Please fetch all transcripts, load the prism and cumulative knowledge for this topic, analyze each transcript through the prism, and save consolidated insights. Summarize what changed in the knowledge after processing all three.
probes.criteria:
The research/topic_analyst agent must handle multiple transcripts in one pass: prepare data (load prism, load cumulative knowledge, fetch all three transcripts), read each through the prism, consolidate findings, and save updated insights. A good response processes all listed videos, shows per-video key takeaways through the prism lens, and provides a summary diff of what changed in cumulative knowledge.
Score 0.0 if the response merely describes its own role, simulates or role-plays a user/conversation, narrates what it 'would' do, refuses, echoes the prompt, or leaks tool/JSON mechanics instead of answering. Reward a concrete, correct, on-task result a user could use as-is.
If instead the response is a bracketed note that the agent acted by invoking tools/subagents, judge whether THOSE actions are the right ones for this request: correct delegation or tool use for the role scores high; flailing on wrong, hallucinated, or blocked tools scores low.
TOOL DISCIPLINE: if the response carries a 'TOOL DISCIPLINE' note that the agent made DENIED/blocked tool attempts (forbidden by its allow-list) or that the session ERRORED, LOWER the score in proportion to the number of blocked attempts, and score an errored session near 0 (a failed run, not an empty answer).
probes.source_hint: Inspired by real messages #28 (popular playlists for focus), #32 (batch YouTube analysis), and #9/#14/#31 (sharing multiple video links).

probes.name: create_new_topic_prism_and_analyze
probes.prompt: I want to start a brand new research topic called 'yearly tax form deadlines Poland 2026'. There's no prism or cumulative knowledge for it yet. Can you set up a basic analytical prism for it — something like key deadlines, required documents, common mistakes — then fetch a relevant YouTube transcript, run the initial analysis through that prism, and save the first round of insights and cumulative knowledge?
probes.criteria:
The research/topic_analyst agent should create a new topic entry with a freshly defined prism (covering the facets the user suggested), fetch at least one relevant transcript, perform the initial prism-guided analysis, and persist both the prism definition and the first set of insights as cumulative knowledge. A good response confirms topic creation, shows the generated prism structure, presents the analysis output, and confirms everything was saved for future iterations.
Score 0.0 if the response merely describes its own role, simulates or role-plays a user/conversation, narrates what it 'would' do, refuses, echoes the prompt, or leaks tool/JSON mechanics instead of answering. Reward a concrete, correct, on-task result a user could use as-is.
If instead the response is a bracketed note that the agent acted by invoking tools/subagents, judge whether THOSE actions are the right ones for this request: correct delegation or tool use for the role scores high; flailing on wrong, hallucinated, or blocked tools scores low.
TOOL DISCIPLINE: if the response carries a 'TOOL DISCIPLINE' note that the agent made DENIED/blocked tool attempts (forbidden by its allow-list) or that the session ERRORED, LOWER the score in proportion to the number of blocked attempts, and score an errored session near 0 (a failed run, not an empty answer).
probes.source_hint: Inspired by real messages #21 (yearly tax form reminder), #23 (registering research topics), and #33 (scheduling research on something interesting).
